'''Fetuli Talanoa''' (born 23 November 1987) is a former [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|wg}} or {{rlp|ce}}. He played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) and for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]]. He announced his retirement in April 2019.

==Background==
Talanoa was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand. He is of Tongan ancestry.

==Playing career==
Talanoa began his playing career for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the NRL<ref>{{cite web |url=https://leagueunlimited.com/news/rabbitohs-re-sign-talanoa/ |title=Rabbitohs re-sign Talanoa |publisher=League Unlimited |date= |access-date=31 May 2022}}</ref> and also played for Souths feeder side The [[North Sydney Bears]] in The [[NSW Cup]].<ref name=SSRA01/>
[[File:Fetuli Talanoa.jpg|thumb|left|Fetuli Talanoa playing for Tonga]]
With Norths, Talanoa played 38 games and scored 14 tries.<ref name=SSRA01>{{Cite web|url=http://www.ssralmanac.com/mobile/pastplayers_bears.htm|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs North Sydney Bears Affiliated Players}}</ref>

In 2014, Talanoa signed for [[Super League]] club [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]. In 2016, he would play in Hull's 12-10 win over the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[2016 Challenge Cup]] final. This was the first time that Hull had won the cup at [[Wembley Stadium]] after 8 previous attempts.<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

In 2017, Hull won the [[2017 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] again with Talanoa scoring a try in the 18-14 win over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

Talanoa announced his immediate retirement from playing on 9 April 2019 after discovering that a pre-season wrist injury which prevented him from making any appearances for Hull F.C. during the first half of the [[Super League XXIV|2019 Super League season]] would require further surgery.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/47874712 |title=Hull FC: Fetuli Talanoa retires ages 31 after latest injury |work=[[BBC Sport]] |date=9 April 2019 |access-date=9 April 2019}}</ref>

==International==
Fetuli has appeared on several occasions for the [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] with his first international appearances coming during the 2006 [[Federation Shield]] competition.

In August 2008, Talanoa was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Tonga|Tonga training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref name="Tug-of-war over Mason">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3935853,00.html|title=Tug-of-war over Mason|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-05|access-date=2008-08-06}}</ref> and in October 2008 he was named in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Tonga|Tonga]] squad.<ref name="Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-08|access-date=2008-10-09}}</ref>

He was named in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|New Zealand training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Kiwis select Sonny Bill">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949034,00.html|title=Kiwis select Sonny Bill|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-08|access-date=2008-08-08}}</ref>

==Honours==
*[[Challenge Cup]]: (2) 2016, 2017
